name: Seven-Point Story Structure
description: A focused 7-beat structure built by working backwards from the resolution. Particularly effective for plotting mysteries, thrillers, and tightly wound narratives.

children.title: Hook
children.synopsis: The starting state — the opposite of the resolution.

scenes.title: Hook
scenes.synopsis: Show the protagonist in a state opposite to where they'll end up.

children.title: Plot Turn 1
children.synopsis: The event that sets the story in motion and introduces the conflict.

scenes.title: Plot Turn 1
scenes.synopsis: Something happens that changes the hero's world and launches the conflict.

children.title: Pinch Point 1
children.synopsis: Apply pressure. Show the antagonist's strength or the stakes.

scenes.title: Pinch Point 1
scenes.synopsis: The villain or opposing force demonstrates their power.

children.title: Midpoint
children.synopsis: The protagonist shifts from reaction to action.

scenes.title: Midpoint
scenes.synopsis: The hero stops running and starts fighting. A fundamental shift in approach.

children.title: Pinch Point 2
children.synopsis: Apply more pressure. The situation seems hopeless.

scenes.title: Pinch Point 2
scenes.synopsis: The antagonist applies maximum pressure. All seems lost.

children.title: Plot Turn 2
children.synopsis: The final piece falls into place. The hero gets the key to winning.

scenes.title: Plot Turn 2
scenes.synopsis: The hero discovers the final piece they need — the power was inside them all along.

children.title: Resolution
children.synopsis: The climax and denouement. The opposite of the hook.

scenes.title: Resolution
scenes.synopsis: The hero confronts the conflict and achieves transformation. Mirror the hook.
